hints: The die is symmetric under v ↔ 7−v, and so is the median.
A symmetric distribution about 3.5 has mean 3.5.
E[median]=7/2.
solution: Replacing each face $v$ by $7-v$ leaves the dice distribution unchanged and maps the median to $7-\text{median}$. So the median is symmetric about $3.5$ and $\mathbb{E}[\text{median}]=\tfrac72.$

hints: Army speed 1, horseman s. Forward trip: 50/(s−1); back trip: 50/(s+1); total time = 50 (army walks 50).
solution: Let the army move at speed $1$ and the horseman at $s$. The forward and back legs take $\tfrac{50}{s-1}$ and $\tfrac{50}{s+1}$, summing to the total time $50$: $\tfrac1{s-1}+\tfrac1{s+1}=1\Rightarrow s^2-2s-1=0\Rightarrow s=1+\sqrt2$. The horseman travels $50s=50(1+\sqrt2)$, so $abc=50\cdot1\cdot2=100.$

hints: Each large loaf becomes two smalls (one eaten immediately, one returned to be eaten later).
Use a 'first ace' argument on the successive appearances of the large loaves to count smalls eaten between them.
Sum the contributions plus the small eaten from each large.
Inscribe the coin (cylinder, radius 1, thickness h) in a concentric sphere; 'side' = the spherical zone between the two faces.
By Archimedes, zone area ∝ height, so P(side)=h/(2R) with R=√(1+(h/2)²).
solution: Inscribe the coin in a concentric sphere; landing on its side corresponds to a spherical zone of height $h$, whose area is proportional to $h$ (Archimedes). So $P(\text{side})=\dfrac{h}{2R}$ with $R=\sqrt{1+(h/2)^2}$. Setting $\dfrac{h}{2\sqrt{1+h^2/4}}=\dfrac13$ gives $9h^2=4+h^2$, so $h^2=\tfrac12$ and $h=\tfrac1{\sqrt2}$, i.e. $k=2.$

hints: Group the 1000 people into bubbles; within a bubble everyone meets everyone over many rounds, while bubbles are paired across rounds.
Arrange so the infection spreads as slowly as possible (the worst case), placing the last healthy person far from the source.
Carefully count the rounds to reach the last person.
The first card fixes the rank; the second must match it.
3 of the remaining 51 cards share the rank.
solution: Whatever the first card is, $3$ of the remaining $51$ cards share its rank, so $P=\tfrac3{51}=\tfrac1{17}.$

hints: T₁₀₀ has mean 25000 and variance 100·250²=6.25×10⁶.
solution: $T_{100}$ has mean $25000$ and variance $100\cdot250^2=6.25\times10^6$. By Chebyshev, $P(T>30000)\le P(|T-25000|>5000)\le\dfrac{6.25\times10^6}{5000^2}=\dfrac14.$

hints: When A finishes (1000m), B is at 800, so v_B/v_A=4/5; similarly v_C/v_B=4/5.
Distance left = 1000−640 = 360 m.
solution: When A finishes, B has gone $800$m, so $v_B/v_A=\tfrac45$; likewise $v_C/v_B=\tfrac45$. Thus $v_C/v_A=\tfrac{16}{25}$, putting C at $640$m. C is $1000-640=360$ meters from finishing.

hints: Inradius r = Area/s. With sides 16,16,24, s=28 and Area=48√7 (Heron).
solution: With $s=28$, Heron gives Area $=\sqrt{28\cdot12\cdot12\cdot4}=48\sqrt7$. The inradius is $r=\tfrac{\text{Area}}{s}=\tfrac{48\sqrt7}{28}=\tfrac{12\sqrt7}{7}$, so the incircle area is $\pi r^2=\dfrac{144\pi}{7}$. Hence $p+q=144+7=151.$

hints: Equal profit means equal gross returns: 3A=4B=11C.
Smallest whole-dollar bets: set each to lcm(3,4,11)=132 ⇒ A=44,B=33,C=12.
Total staked 89, return 132, profit 132−89=43.
solution: For equal profit in every outcome the gross returns must match: $3A=4B=11C$. The smallest whole-dollar bets set each to $\mathrm{lcm}(3,4,11)=132$, giving $A=44,B=33,C=12$ (total $\$89$). The guaranteed return is $\$132$, so the profit is $132-89=\$43.$

hints: To make the sum uniform on 1–12 with a normal 1–6 die, the other die must shift by 0 or 6.
Faces {0,0,0,6,6,6} give sums 1–6 and 7–12 each with probability 1/12.
Sum of faces = 3·0+3·6 = 18.
solution: For the sum to be uniform on $1$–$12$, the illegible die must have faces $\{0,0,0,6,6,6\}$: adding $0$ gives sums $1$–$6$ and adding $6$ gives $7$–$12$, each value with probability $\tfrac1{12}$. The face sum is $3(0)+3(6)=18.$
